# QuickFIX/Go Feature Inventory (`thrdpty/quickfix`)

A vendored copy of `github.com/quickfixgo/quickfix` (full-featured Go FIX engine). Note: all files are git-ignored (vendored), so I bypassed ignore filters with `find`/`ls`.

## 1. Top-level directory structure
- **Root package `quickfix`** (~80 `.go` files): core engine, states, message model, validation, acceptor/initiator.
- **`config/`** — config key constants (`configuration.go`).
- **`datadictionary/`** — XML dictionary parsing/model (`build.go`, `datadictionary.go`, `xml.go`).
- **`store/`** — message store backends: `file/`, `memory/`, `mongo/`, `sql/`.
- **`log/`** — log backends: `composite/`, `file/`, `mongo/`, `screen/`, `sql/`.
- **`internal/`** — `event.go`, `event_timer.go`, `time_range.go`, `session_settings.go`, `testsuite/store_suite.go` (shared store conformance suite).
- **`spec/`** — bundled dictionaries: FIX40–FIX44, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2, FIXT11.
- **`cmd/generate-fix`** — code-generation tool.
- **`_sql/`** — DB schemas for `mssql`, `mysql`, `oracle`, `postgresql`, `sqlite3` (with `embed.go`).
- **`_test/`, `_test_data/`**, `.devcontainer`, `.github`.

## 2. Main package — key files & responsibilities
- `application.go` — `Application` interface: `OnCreate`, `OnLogon`, `OnLogout`, `ToAdmin`, `ToApp`, `FromAdmin`, `FromApp`.
- `session.go` (24KB) — core session logic (send/recv, logon/logout handling, seq nums, gap fill/resend).
- `session_state.go` — the state machine (`stateMachine` + `sessionState` interface).
- `session_factory.go` — builds sessions from settings (maps config → session fields).
- `acceptor.go` / `initiator.go` / `dialer.go` / `connection.go` / `tls.go` — transport.
- `message.go`, `field_map.go`, `field.go`, `tag.go`, `tag_value.go`, `repeating_group.go`, `parser.go`, `msg_type.go`, `begin_string.go` — message model & parsing.
- `validation.go` — `Validator`/`ValidatorSettings` (fixValidator + fixtValidator).
- `message_router.go`, `registry.go` — routing + global session registry.
- `errors.go`, `session_rejects.go` — reject/error model.
- `fix_*.go` — typed field types: `FIXBoolean`, `FIXInt`, `FIXFloat`, `FIXDecimal`, `FIXUDecimal` (unsigned), `FIXString`, `FIXBytes`, `FIXUTCTimestamp`.
- `store.go`/`memory_store.go`, `log.go`/`null_log.go` — store & log interfaces.
- State files: `latent_state.go`, `not_session_time.go`, `logon_state.go`, `logout_state.go`, `in_session.go`, `resend_state.go`, `pending_timeout.go`.

## 3. Session states & transitions (`session_state.go`)
`sessionState` interface methods: `FixMsgIn`, `Timeout`, `IsLoggedOn`, `IsConnected`, `IsSessionTime`, `ShutdownNow`, `Stop`, `Stringer`.

Embedded trait hierarchy:
- `inSessionTime` → `connected` → `connectedNotLoggedOn` (→ `logonState`, `logoutState`); `connected` → `loggedOn` (→ `inSession`, `resendState`).
- Standalone: `latentState` (disconnected), `notSessionTime`, `pendingTimeout` (wraps another state).

`stateMachine` transitions: `Start`→`latentState`+`CheckSessionTime`; `Connect`→`logonState` (initiator sends logon, arms `LogonTimeout`); `Stop` sets `pendingStop`; `Disconnected`→`latentState`; `Incoming` parses via dictionary then `fixMsgIn`; `Timeout`→`state.Timeout`; `CheckSessionTime` → `notSessionTime` when out of range, `latentState` when back in range, and **session reset** when crossing a day boundary (`!IsInSameRange(CreationTime, now)`). `CheckResetTime` implements the **ResetSeqTime** feature (sends a logon-with-reset at a configured wall-clock time while staying connected).

Per-state flows: `logonState` handles `RejectLogon`/`targetTooLow`/`targetTooHigh`; `inSession` dispatches logon/logout/resendRequest/sequenceReset/reject/testRequest/heartbeat, →`logoutState` on logout; `resendState` does chunked resend + message stash + gap-fill handling; `logoutState.Timeout` & `pendingTimeout.Timeout(PeerTimeout)` → `latentState`.

## 4. Store implementations
`MessageStore` interface (`store.go`): `NextSender/TargetMsgSeqNum`, `Incr…`, `SetNext…`, `CreationTime`/`SetCreationTime`, `SaveMessage`, **`SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`** (atomic), `GetMessages`, **`IterateMessages`** (streaming callback), `Refresh`, `Reset`, `Close`. Backends: **memory, file, sql, mongo**; `FileStoreSync` toggle; configurable SQL table names. Shared conformance suite in `internal/testsuite/store_suite.go`.

## 5. Config keys (`config/configuration.go`)
**Session:** BeginString, Sender/TargetCompID+SubID+LocationID, SessionQualifier, DefaultApplVerID, StartTime/EndTime, StartDay/EndDay, **Weekdays**, TimeZone, TimeStampPrecision (SECONDS/MILLIS/MICROS/NANOS), ResetOnLogon, RefreshOnLogon, ResetOnLogout, ResetOnDisconnect, **ResetSeqTime**.
**Validation:** DataDictionary, TransportDataDictionary, AppDataDictionary (supports per-ApplVerID prefix `AppDataDictionary.FIX.4.4`), RejectInvalidMessage, AllowUnknownMessageFields, CheckUserDefinedFields, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, CheckLatency, MaxLatency, **InChanCapacity**.
**Initiator:** ReconnectInterval, LogoutTimeout, LogonTimeout, HeartBtInt, SocketConnectHost/Port (+ numbered failover alternates), SocketTimeout, **ProxyType/Host/Port/User/Password** (SOCKS).
**Acceptor:** SocketAcceptHost/Port, HeartBtIntOverride, **UseTCPProxy**, **DynamicSessions**, **DynamicQualifier**.
**Security/TLS:** SocketPrivateKeyFile/CertificateFile/CAFile, **SocketPrivateKeyBytes/CertificateBytes/CABytes** (inline PEM), SocketInsecureSkipVerify, SocketServerName (SNI), SocketMinimumTLSVersion, SocketUseSSL.
**Logging:** FileLogPath, SQLLog*, MongoLog*.
**Storage:** PersistMessages, FileStorePath, FileStoreSync, SQLStore* (incl. **SQLStoreMessagesTableName/SQLStoreSessionsTableName**), MongoStore*.
**Misc:** ResendRequestChunkSize, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed (tag 369), EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum (tag 789).

## 6. Dictionary support
`datadictionary.DataDictionary` models FIXType/Major/Minor/ServicePack, `FieldTypeByTag/Name`, `Messages`, `ComponentTypes`, `Header`, `Trailer`. `MessagePart` = Field / RepeatingGroup / Component with required-flag tracking. XML parsing in `xml.go`/`build.go`. Validation split into `fixValidator` (single dict, FIX≤4.4) and `fixtValidator` (transport + app dict, FIXT1.1+). Bundled specs cover FIX4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT1.1.

## 7. Transport: acceptor / initiator
**Acceptor** (`acceptor.go`): multi-listener (`map[string]net.Listener`), per-session host/port; **DynamicSessions**/DynamicQualifier (unconfigured peers); **`ConnectionValidator`** hook (custom auth/mTLS/IP binding); **`NewListenerCallback`** (custom listener creation); `SetTLSConfig`/`SetConnectionValidator`/`SetNewListenerCallback`; **UseTCPProxy** (HAProxy/ELB PROXY protocol via `go-proxyproto`); `RemoteAddr` lookup; `dynamicSessionsLoop`.
**Initiator** (`initiator.go`/`dialer.go`): per-session goroutines + reconnect loop using `ReconnectInterval`; per-session TLS; **SOCKS proxy** (`golang.org/x/net/proxy` via `loadDialerConfig`); numbered `SocketConnectHost/Port<n>` failover alternates; `SocketTimeout` for TLS handshake; `waitForInSessionTime`/`waitForReconnectInterval`.

## 8. Features QuickFIX/Go has that QF/J may lack (parity candidates)
These are notable/candidate differences worth verifying against QF/J:
- **`ResetSeqTime`** — scheduled wall-clock seq-num reset via logon while staying connected (`CheckResetTime`).
- **`InChanCapacity`** — bounded incoming-message channel buffer (backpressure).
- **`IterateMessages`** + **`SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`** — streaming and atomic store ops.
- **`DynamicSessions`/`DynamicQualifier`** — acceptor admits unconfigured peers; disambiguates duplicate SenderCompIDs.
- **`ConnectionValidator`** & **`NewListenerCallback`** acceptor hooks (custom auth/mTLS/listener).
- **SOCKS proxy** support for initiators (`ProxyType=socks`).
- **TCP PROXY protocol** (`UseTCPProxy`) for HAProxy/ELB.
- **Inline PEM bytes** config (`SocketPrivateKeyBytes`/`CertificateBytes`/`CABytes`) — not just file paths.
- **Decimal field types** (`FIXDecimal`/`FIXUDecimal`) and `FIXBytes`.
- **Configurable SQL table names** (`SQLStoreMessagesTableName`/`SQLStoreSessionsTableName`).
- **NANOsecond timestamp precision** (`TimeStampPrecision=NANOS`).
- **`Weekdays`** setting for daily sessions restricted to specific weekdays.
- **`cmd/generate-fix`** codegen tool.

Standard features shared with QF/J (for the parity matrix): FIX 4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT1.1; file/sql/mongo/memory stores; file/sql/mongo/screen/composite logs; repeating groups/components; FIXT transport/app dictionary split with per-ApplVerID app dictionaries; `ResendRequestChunkSize`; tags 369/789; reset-on-logon/logout/disconnect; refresh-on-logon (hot failover); session-time/week-long sessions; TLS/mTLS.

  - `next_steps`: Cross-check the §8 candidate list against QuickFIX/J's actual feature set (config keys, store/log interfaces, acceptor hooks) to confirm which are true gaps vs. parity. For the truefix port, prioritize matching: the session state machine & transitions, the MessageStore interface (especially IterateMessages/SaveMessageAndIncr), config key surface area, datadictionary model, and the acceptor/initiator extension hooks.
- **Result**: Task completed. Output: Status: success
  
  Result:
  # QuickFIX/Go Feature Inventory (`thrdpty/quickfix`)
  
  A vendored copy of `github.com/quickfixgo/quickfix` (full-featured Go FIX engine). Note: all files are git-ignored (vendored), so I bypassed ignore filters with `find`/`ls`.
  
  ## 1. Top-level directory structure
  - **Root package `quickfix`** (~80 `.go` files): core engine, states, message model, validation, acceptor/initiator.
  - **`config/`** — config key constants (`configuration.go`).
  - **`datadictionary/`** — XML dictionary parsing/model (`build.go`, `datadictionary.go`, `xml.go`).
  - **`store/`** — message store backends: `file/`, `memory/`, `mongo/`, `sql/`.
  - **`log/`** — log backends: `composite/`, `file/`, `mongo/`, `screen/`, `sql/`.
  - **`internal/`** — `event.go`, `event_timer.go`, `time_range.go`, `session_settings.go`, `testsuite/store_suite.go` (shared store conformance suite).
  - **`spec/`** — bundled dictionaries: FIX40–FIX44, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2, FIXT11.
  - **`cmd/generate-fix`** — code-generation tool.
  - **`_sql/`** — DB schemas for `mssql`, `mysql`, `oracle`, `postgresql`, `sqlite3` (with `embed.go`).
  - **`_test/`, `_test_data/`**, `.devcontainer`, `.github`.
  
  ## 2. Main package — key files & responsibilities
  - `application.go` — `Application` interface: `OnCreate`, `OnLogon`, `OnLogout`, `ToAdmin`, `ToApp`, `FromAdmin`, `FromApp`.
  - `session.go` (24KB) — core session logic (send/recv, logon/logout handling, seq nums, gap fill/resend).
  - `session_state.go` — the state machine (`stateMachine` + `sessionState` interface).
  - `session_factory.go` — builds sessions from settings (maps config → session fields).
  - `acceptor.go` / `initiator.go` / `dialer.go` / `connection.go` / `tls.go` — transport.
  - `message.go`, `field_map.go`, `field.go`, `tag.go`, `tag_value.go`, `repeating_group.go`, `parser.go`, `msg_type.go`, `begin_string.go` — message model & parsing.
  - `validation.go` — `Validator`/`ValidatorSettings` (fixValidator + fixtValidator).
  - `message_router.go`, `registry.go` — routing + global session registry.
  - `errors.go`, `session_rejects.go` — reject/error model.
  - `fix_*.go` — typed field types: `FIXBoolean`, `FIXInt`, `FIXFloat`, `FIXDecimal`, `FIXUDecimal` (unsigned), `FIXString`, `FIXBytes`, `FIXUTCTimestamp`.
  - `store.go`/`memory_store.go`, `log.go`/`null_log.go` — store & log interfaces.
  - State files: `latent_state.go`, `not_session_time.go`, `logon_state.go`, `logout_state.go`, `in_session.go`, `resend_state.go`, `pending_timeout.go`.
  
  ## 3. Session states & transitions (`session_state.go`)
  `sessionState` interface methods: `FixMsgIn`, `Timeout`, `IsLoggedOn`, `IsConnected`, `IsSessionTime`, `ShutdownNow`, `Stop`, `Stringer`.
  
  Embedded trait hierarchy:
  - `inSessionTime` → `connected` → `connectedNotLoggedOn` (→ `logonState`, `logoutState`); `connected` → `loggedOn` (→ `inSession`, `resendState`).
  - Standalone: `latentState` (disconnected), `notSessionTime`, `pendingTimeout` (wraps another state).
  
  `stateMachine` transitions: `Start`→`latentState`+`CheckSessionTime`; `Connect`→`logonState` (initiator sends logon, arms `LogonTimeout`); `Stop` sets `pendingStop`; `Disconnected`→`latentState`; `Incoming` parses via dictionary then `fixMsgIn`; `Timeout`→`state.Timeout`; `CheckSessionTime` → `notSessionTime` when out of range, `latentState` when back in range, and **session reset** when crossing a day boundary (`!IsInSameRange(CreationTime, now)`). `CheckResetTime` implements the **ResetSeqTime** feature (sends a logon-with-reset at a configured wall-clock time while staying connected).
  
  Per-state flows: `logonState` handles `RejectLogon`/`targetTooLow`/`targetTooHigh`; `inSession` dispatches logon/logout/resendRequest/sequenceReset/reject/testRequest/heartbeat, →`logoutState` on logout; `resendState` does chunked resend + message stash + gap-fill handling; `logoutState.Timeout` & `pendingTimeout.Timeout(PeerTimeout)` → `latentState`.
  
  ## 4. Store implementations
  `MessageStore` interface (`store.go`): `NextSender/TargetMsgSeqNum`, `Incr…`, `SetNext…`, `CreationTime`/`SetCreationTime`, `SaveMessage`, **`SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`** (atomic), `GetMessages`, **`IterateMessages`** (streaming callback), `Refresh`, `Reset`, `Close`. Backends: **memory, file, sql, mongo**; `FileStoreSync` toggle; configurable SQL table names. Shared conformance suite in `internal/testsuite/store_suite.go`.
  
  ## 5. Config keys (`config/configuration.go`)
  **Session:** BeginString, Sender/TargetCompID+SubID+LocationID, SessionQualifier, DefaultApplVerID, StartTime/EndTime, StartDay/EndDay, **Weekdays**, TimeZone, TimeStampPrecision (SECONDS/MILLIS/MICROS/NANOS), ResetOnLogon, RefreshOnLogon, ResetOnLogout, ResetOnDisconnect, **ResetSeqTime**.
  **Validation:** DataDictionary, TransportDataDictionary, AppDataDictionary (supports per-ApplVerID prefix `AppDataDictionary.FIX.4.4`), RejectInvalidMessage, AllowUnknownMessageFields, CheckUserDefinedFields, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, CheckLatency, MaxLatency, **InChanCapacity**.
  **Initiator:** ReconnectInterval, LogoutTimeout, LogonTimeout, HeartBtInt, SocketConnectHost/Port (+ numbered failover alternates), SocketTimeout, **ProxyType/Host/Port/User/Password** (SOCKS).
  **Acceptor:** SocketAcceptHost/Port, HeartBtIntOverride, **UseTCPProxy**, **DynamicSessions**, **DynamicQualifier**.
  **Security/TLS:** SocketPrivateKeyFile/CertificateFile/CAFile, **SocketPrivateKeyBytes/CertificateBytes/CABytes** (inline PEM), SocketInsecureSkipVerify, SocketServerName (SNI), SocketMinimumTLSVersion, SocketUseSSL.
  **Logging:** FileLogPath, SQLLog*, MongoLog*.
  **Storage:** PersistMessages, FileStorePath, FileStoreSync, SQLStore* (incl. **SQLStoreMessagesTableName/SQLStoreSessionsTableName**), MongoStore*.
  **Misc:** ResendRequestChunkSize, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed (tag 369), EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum (tag 789).
  
  ## 6. Dictionary support
  `datadictionary.DataDictionary` models FIXType/Major/Minor/ServicePack, `FieldTypeByTag/Name`, `Messages`, `ComponentTypes`, `Header`, `Trailer`. `MessagePart` = Field / RepeatingGroup / Component with required-flag tracking. XML parsing in `xml.go`/`build.go`. Validation split into `fixValidator` (single dict, FIX≤4.4) and `fixtValidator` (transport + app dict, FIXT1.1+). Bundled specs cover FIX4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT1.1.
  
  ## 7. Transport: acceptor / initiator
  **Acceptor** (`acceptor.go`): multi-listener (`map[string]net.Listener`), per-session host/port; **DynamicSessions**/DynamicQualifier (unconfigured peers); **`ConnectionValidator`** hook (custom auth/mTLS/IP binding); **`NewListenerCallback`** (custom listener creation); `SetTLSConfig`/`SetConnectionValidator`/`SetNewListenerCallback`; **UseTCPProxy** (HAProxy/ELB PROXY protocol via `go-proxyproto`); `RemoteAddr` lookup; `dynamicSessionsLoop`.
  **Initiator** (`initiator.go`/`dialer.go`): per-session goroutines + reconnect loop using `ReconnectInterval`; per-session TLS; **SOCKS proxy** (`golang.org/x/net/proxy` via `loadDialerConfig`); numbered `SocketConnectHost/Port<n>` failover alternates; `SocketTimeout` for TLS handshake; `waitForInSessionTime`/`waitForReconnectInterval`.
  
  ## 8. Features QuickFIX/Go has that QF/J may lack (parity candidates)
  These are notable/candidate differences worth verifying against QF/J:
  - **`ResetSeqTime`** — scheduled wall-clock seq-num reset via logon while staying connected (`CheckResetTime`).
  - **`InChanCapacity`** — bounded incoming-message channel buffer (backpressure).
  - **`IterateMessages`** + **`SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`** — streaming and atomic store ops.
  - **`DynamicSessions`/`DynamicQualifier`** — acceptor admits unconfigured peers; disambiguates duplicate SenderCompIDs.
  - **`ConnectionValidator`** & **`NewListenerCallback`** acceptor hooks (custom auth/mTLS/listener).
  - **SOCKS proxy** support for initiators (`ProxyType=socks`).
  - **TCP PROXY protocol** (`UseTCPProxy`) for HAProxy/ELB.
  - **Inline PEM bytes** config (`SocketPrivateKeyBytes`/`CertificateBytes`/`CABytes`) — not just file paths.
  - **Decimal field types** (`FIXDecimal`/`FIXUDecimal`) and `FIXBytes`.
  - **Configurable SQL table names** (`SQLStoreMessagesTableName`/`SQLStoreSessionsTableName`).
  - **NANOsecond timestamp precision** (`TimeStampPrecision=NANOS`).
  - **`Weekdays`** setting for daily sessions restricted to specific weekdays.
  - **`cmd/generate-fix`** codegen tool.
  
  Standard features shared with QF/J (for the parity matrix): FIX 4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT1.1; file/sql/mongo/memory stores; file/sql/mongo/screen/composite logs; repeating groups/components; FIXT transport/app dictionary split with per-ApplVerID app dictionaries; `ResendRequestChunkSize`; tags 369/789; reset-on-logon/logout/disconnect; refresh-on-logon (hot failover); session-time/week-long sessions; TLS/mTLS.
  
  Next steps:
  Cross-check the §8 candidate list against QuickFIX/J's actual feature set (config keys, store/log interfaces, acceptor hooks) to confirm which are true gaps vs. parity. For the truefix port, prioritize matching: the session state machine & transitions, the MessageStore interface (especially IterateMessages/SaveMessageAndIncr), config key surface area, datadictionary model, and the acceptor/initiator extension hooks.

**✅ complete_task** (`complete_task`)
- **Status**: success
- **Call ID**: `call_f32c72112e7343c7975b02a2`
- **Timestamp**: 6/29/2026, 10:06:09 PM

- **Arguments**:
  - `status`: success
  - `result`: ## QuickFIX/J Feature Inventory (for parity comparison)

Explored `/Users/jiayin/workspace/dev/dev/rust/truefix/thrdpty/quickfixj` (QFJ 3.x — a vendored git repo, git-ignored by the parent project). The codebase is a multi-module Maven build. Below is a comprehensive feature inventory organized by the requested categories.

### 1. Top-Level Module Structure
Modules: `quickfixj-all` (uberjar), `quickfixj-base` (version-agnostic core: Field/Message/Dictionary), `quickfixj-core` (engine: Session/transport/store/log), `quickfixj-dictgenerator` (codegen), `quickfixj-orchestration` (FIX Orchestra → dictionary for FIX Latest), `quickfixj-messages` (typed message libs per FIX version), `quickfixj-examples`, `quickfixj-distribution`, `quickfixj-perf-test`, `quickfixj-stress-test`. Plus `docs/`, `customising-quickfixj.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `pom.xml`, `mvnw`.

### 2. quickfixj-core/src/main/java/quickfix/ — ~73 top-level classes
Key interfaces/classes: `Connector`, `Acceptor`, `Initiator`, `Application`, `ApplicationExtended` (+ FunctionalAdapter/Adapter variants), `Session`, `SessionState`, `SessionSettings`, `SessionID`, `SessionFactory`/`DefaultSessionFactory`, `SessionSchedule`/`DefaultSessionSchedule`/`DefaultSessionScheduleFactory`, `Responder`, `MessageStore`/`MessageStoreFactory`, `MessageQueue`/`MessageQueueFactory`, `Log`/`LogFactory`/`AbstractLog`, `DataDictionaryProvider`/`DefaultDataDictionaryProvider`, `DefaultMessageFactory`, `MessageCracker`, `MessageSessionUtils`, `RejectLogon`, `SessionStateListener`, `ExecutorFactory`, listener interfaces (`FromAppListener`, `FromAdminListener`, `ToAppListener`).

quickfixj-base (org.quickfixj + quickfix): `Field`, `FieldMap`, `Message`, `MessageComponent`, `Group`, `MessageFactory`, `DataDictionary`, `Dictionary`, `ValidationSettings`, `FixVersions`, `SessionID`, `DayConverter`, `SystemTime`/`SystemTimeSource`, `CharsetSupport`, field type classes (`StringField`, `IntField`, `BooleanField`, `CharField`, `DecimalField`, `DoubleField`, `DateField`, `UtcTimeStampField`, `UtcTimeOnlyField`, `UtcDateOnlyField`, `BytesField`), and `field/converter/` package (Boolean, Char, Decimal, Double, Int, CharArray, UtcTimestamp/TimeOnly/DateOnly converters).

### 3. Session Layer
- **Session.java** is the central state machine (~2900+ lines). Key methods: `logon()`, `logout(reason)`, `next(Message)` (per-message dispatch), `next()` (timer/heartbeat tick), `disconnect(reason, logError)`, `reset()`, `send(message, allowPosDup)`, `isLoggedOn()`, `getExpectedSenderNum()`. Handles resend logic, sequence validation, test requests, logon/logout handshake.
- **SessionState.java** is a `final class` (NOT an enum) holding state as boolean flags: `logonSent`, `logonReceived`, `logoutSent`, `logoutReceived`, plus `logonTimeoutMs` (default 10000), `logoutTimeoutMs` (default 2000), `testRequestCounter`, `lastSentTime`, `lastReceivedTime`, `testRequestDelayMultiplier`. Holds `MessageStore`, `MessageQueue`, `Log`, and separate `ReentrantLock`s for sender/target seq nums. Inner classes: `ResendRange`, `NullLog`. Derived predicates: `isLogonSent`, `isLogonReceived`, `isLogoutSent`, `isLogoutReceived`, logon/logout timeout checks.
- **SessionSettings.java** configures from INI-like files with `[default]` and `[session]` sections. Public session-identity constants only: `BeginString`, `SenderCompID`, `SenderSubID`, `SenderLocationID`, `TargetCompID`, `TargetSubID`, `TargetLocationID`, `SessionQualifier`.

### 4. Transport Layer (MINA-based)
- Public connectors: `SocketAcceptor`, `SocketInitiator`, `ThreadedSocketAcceptor`, `ThreadedSocketInitiator`.
- `quickfix/mina` package: `AbstractIoHandler`, `CompositeIoFilterChainBuilder`, `EventHandlingStrategy` (with `SingleThreadedEventHandlingStrategy` and `ThreadPerSessionEventHandlingStrategy` for single-vs-thread-per-session message dispatch), `IoSessionResponder`, `NetworkingOptions`, `ProtocolFactory`, `QueueTracker`/`QueueTrackers`/`WatermarkTracker` (backpressure/queue management), `SessionConnector`, `CriticalProtocolCodecException`.
- `mina/acceptor`: `AbstractSocketAcceptor`, `AcceptorIoHandler`, `AcceptorSessionProvider`, **`DynamicAcceptorSessionProvider`** (dynamic session creation on incoming logon).
- `mina/initiator`: `AbstractSocketInitiator`, `InitiatorIoHandler`, **`InitiatorProxyIoHandler`** (proxy support), `IoSessionInitiator` (manages reconnect interval array + multiple connect hosts/ports), `ConnectException`.
- `mina/message`: `FIXMessageDecoder`, `FIXMessageEncoder`, `FIXProtocolCodecFactory`, `PatternMatcher`.
- `mina/ssl`: `AcceptorSslFilter`, `InitiatorSslFilter`, `SSLConfig`, `SSLContextFactory`, `SSLSupport`, `X509TrustManagerWrapper`.

### 5. Message Store Layer
`MessageStore` interface + factories: `MemoryStore`, `FileStore` (+ `FileStoreSync`, `FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs`), `CachedFileStore` (in-memory cache over file), `JdbcStore` (DB-backed, with `JdbcUtil`, `JdbcSetting`, configurable table names + connection pool), `SleepycatStore` (Berkeley DB / Oracle JE), `NoopStore` (stateless/no persistence). Plus `MessageQueue` abstraction with `InMemoryMessageQueue` and `BoundInMemoryMessageQueue` (bounded queue for backpressure) and their factories.

### 6. Log Layer
`Log` interface + `AbstractLog` base. Implementations: `ScreenLog` (stdout/stderr), `FileLog` (separate event/incoming/outgoing files), `JdbcLog` (DB tables, configurable), `SLF4JLog` (delegates to SLF4J with per-category routing: event/error/incoming/outgoing categories), `CompositeLog` (multiplex to multiple logs). Plus `LocationAwareLogFactory`, `CompositeLogFactory`, `LogUtil`.

### 7. Dictionary / Message Model
- `DataDictionary` (in base): parses FIX XML dictionaries, validates fields/groups/components, msg-type/tag lookups, field-type checking, required-field validation, custom/UDF support.
- `Message` (base): FIX message with header/body/trailer `FieldMap`s, group iteration, parsing/serialization, header field handling, reverse routing.
- `Field`/`FieldMap`/`Group`/`MessageComponent`: typed field hierarchy, ordered field storage, repeating groups, components.
- `MessageCracker`: visitor base class auto-generating `onMessage(...)` overloads per typed message (used by Application callbacks).
- `MessageFactory`/`DefaultMessageFactory`: creates typed messages by BeginString + MsgType, version-aware.
- `DataDictionaryProvider`/`DefaultDataDictionaryProvider`: per-transport/per-app dictionary selection (needed for FIXT 1.1 session/app split).
- `ValidationSettings`, `MessageUtils`, `MessageSessionUtils`.

### 8. Codegen / Typed Messages / Versions
- `quickfixj-dictgenerator` module: `Generator`, `Message`, `Field`, `Component`, `ComponentComponent`, `ComponentField`, `Enum`, `MsgType`, `MsgTypeField`, `MsgTypeComponent`, `Repository` — generates typed Field/Component/Message Java classes from FIX XML dictionaries.
- `quickfixj-codegenerator` Maven plugin (and Gradle usage documented in `customising-quickfixj.md`) — `MessageCodeGenerator` with `Task` (dictFile, messagePackage, fieldPackage, outputBaseDirectory).
- Supported FIX versions (each a `quickfixj-messages-*` module): **FIX 4.0, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 5.0, 5.0sp1, 5.0sp2, FIX Latest, FIXT 1.1**, plus `quickfixj-messages-all` (aggregate).
- FIX 4.0–5.0sp2 generated from QuickFIX XML dictionaries; **FIX Latest generated from a FIX Orchestra repository** via `quickfixj-orchestration` module. `generator.decimal` build property toggles `BigDecimal` vs `double` for Qty/decimal fields. Custom Rules-of-Engagement supported by editing dictionaries/orchestration.

### 9. Acceptance Test Definitions
Located at `quickfixj-core/src/test/resources/quickfix/test/acceptance/definitions/` — **481 `.def` scenario files**, organized by version subdirs (`fix40`, `fix41`, `fix42`, `fix43`, `fix44`, `fix50`, `fixLatest`, plus `future/server/fix43`). Scenario categories: `server/` (core protocol: logon/logout, seqnum gaps, resend, reject, garbled msgs, comp-id checks, test requests, session reset — e.g. `1a_ValidLogon`, `2b_MsgSeqNumTooHigh`, `14a_BadField`, `20_SimultaneousResendRequest`, `ReverseRoute`, `SessionReset`), `lastMsgSeqNumProcessed/`, `nextExpectedMsgSeqNum/`, `rejectGarbledMessages/`, `resendRequestChunkSize/` (chunked resend, gap-fill vs resend), `timestamps/` (higher-resolution timestamps), `validateChecksum/`. Java harness under `src/test/java/quickfix/test/acceptance/` (+ `resynch/`).

### 10. Config Keys (SessionSettings)
Config keys are NOT centralized — they live as `SETTING_*` string constants across classes. Comprehensive set found:
- **Session (Session.java)**: `HeartBtInt`, `CheckLatency`, `CheckCompID`, `MaxLatency`, `TestRequestDelayMultiplier`, `HeartBeatTimeoutMultiplier`, `NonStopSession`, `StartDay`, `EndDay`, `TimeZone`, `StartTime`, `EndTime`, `Weekdays`, `UseDataDictionary`, `DataDictionary`, `TransportDataDictionary`, `AppDataDictionary`, `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder`, `ValidateUnorderedGroupFields`, `FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter`, `ValidateFieldsHaveValues`, `ValidateIncomingMessage`, `LogonTimeout`, `LogoutTimeout`, `LogonTag`, `ResetOnLogout`, `ValidateSequenceNumbers`, `ResetOnDisconnect`, `ResetOnError`, `DisconnectOnError`, `TimeStampPrecision`, `ValidateUserDefinedFields`, `ResetOnLogon`, `Description`, `RefreshOnLogon`, `SendRedundantResendRequests`, `PersistMessages`, `ClosedResendInterval`, `AllowUnknownMsgFields`, `DefaultApplVerID`, `DisableHeartBeatCheck`, `EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed`, `EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, `RejectGarbledMessage`, `RejectInvalidMessage`, `RejectMessageOnUnhandledException`, `RequiresOrigSendingTime`, `ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore`, `AllowedRemoteAddresses`, `LogMessageWhenSessionNotFound`, `ResendRequestChunkSize`, `MaxScheduledWriteRequests`, `ValidateChecksum`, `AllowPosDup`.
- **SessionFactory**: `ConnectionType` (initiator/acceptor), `ContinueInitializationOnError`.
- **Acceptor**: `SocketAcceptProtocol`, `SocketAcceptPort`, `SocketAcceptAddress`, `AcceptorTemplate`.
- **Initiator**: `ReconnectInterval`, `SocketConnectProtocol`, `SocketConnectHost`, `SocketConnectPort`, `SocketConnectTimeout`, `SocketLocalHost`, `SocketLocalPort`, `ProxyType/ProxyVersion/ProxyHost/ProxyPort/ProxyUser/ProxyPassword/ProxyDomain/ProxyWorkstation`, `DynamicSession`.
- **NetworkingOptions**: `SocketKeepAlive`, `SocketOobInline`, `SocketReceiveBufferSize`, `SocketReuseAddress`, `SocketSendBufferSize`, `SocketLinger`, `SocketTcpNoDelay`, `SocketTrafficClass`, `SocketSynchronousWrites`, `SocketSynchronousWriteTimeout`.
- **FileStore**: `FileStorePath`, `FileStoreSync`, `FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs`.
- **FileLog**: `FileLogPath`, `FileIncludeMilliseconds`, `FileIncludeTimeStampForMessages`, `FileLogHeartbeats`.
- **SLF4JLog**: `SLF4JLogEventCategory`, `SLF4JLogErrorEventCategory`, `SLF4JLogIncomingMessageCategory`, `SLF4JLogOutgoingMessageCategory`, `SLF4JLogPrependSessionID`, `SLF4JLogHeartbeats`.
- **JdbcSetting**: `JdbcDriver`, `JdbcURL`, `JdbcUser`, `JdbcPassword`, `JdbcStoreMessagesTableName`, `JdbcStoreSessionsTableName`, `JdbcDataSourceName`, `JndiContextFactory`, `JndiProviderURL`, `JdbcLogHeartBeats`, `JdbcLogOutgoingTable`, `JdbcLogIncomingTable`, `JdbcLogEventTable`, `JdbcSessionIdDefaultPropertyValue`, plus pool sizing (`JdbcMaxActiveConnection`, `JdbcMinIdleConnection`, `JdbcMaxConnectionLifeTime`, `JdbcConnectionTimeout`, `JdbcConnectionIdleTimeout`, `JdbcConnectionKeepaliveTime`, `JdbcConnectionTestQuery`).
- **SSL**: `SSLSupport.SETTING_USE_SSL` (`UseSSL`) plus keystore/truststore/cipher/protocol settings in `SSLSupport`/`SSLConfig` (exact key strings not fully enumerated — see gap below).

### 11. Examples (quickfixj-examples)
- **executor**: minimal acceptor — `Executor` + `Application` + `MarketDataProvider`; auto-fills any incoming NewOrderSingle with an ExecutionReport (sells to any buy).
- **banzai**: Swing-GUI order-entry **initiator** client — `Banzai`/`BanzaiApplication` + UI (`BanzaiFrame`, `OrderEntryPanel`, `OrderTableModel`, `ExecutionTableModel`), supports New/Cancel/Replace, tracks orders/executions, `OrderSide`/`OrderType`/`OrderTIF` enums.
- **ordermatch**: a simple in-memory **matching engine** — `Application`, `Main`, `Market`, `Order`, `OrderMatcher`, `IdGenerator`; matches orders between multiple sessions.

### 12. SSL/TLS Support
Dedicated `mina/ssl` package: `SSLSupport` (config keys), `SSLConfig` (holds keystore/truststore/protocols/ciphers), `SSLContextFactory` (builds `SSLContext`), `InitiatorSslFilter` + `AcceptorSslFilter` (MINA SSL filters for each direction), `X509TrustManagerWrapper` (custom trust manager). Enabled per-session via `UseSSL=true`; acceptor groups sessions by SSL config + bind address in `AcceptorSocketDescriptor`.

### 13. Scheduling (Session Schedule)
- `SessionSchedule` interface: `isSessionTime()`, `isSameSession(time1, time2)`, `isNonStopSession()`.
- `DefaultSessionSchedule` supports **four modes**: non-stop (`NonStopSession=true`), daily (`StartTime`/`EndTime` only), weekly (`StartDay`/`EndDay` + times), and **multi-weekday** (`Weekdays=Sun,Mon,...` comma list parsed via `DayConverter`). Uses `TimeEndPoint`/`TimeInterval` inner classes with day-of-week + time + TimeZone. `DefaultSessionScheduleFactory` builds from settings. Settings: `StartTime`, `EndTime`, `StartDay`, `EndDay`, `Weekdays`, `TimeZone`, `NonStopSession`.

### 14. Reconnect, Dynamic Sessions, Proxy
- **Reconnect**: `ReconnectInterval` accepts a comma-separated list → `int[]` of escalating intervals; `IoSessionInitiator` cycles through `reconnectIntervalInMillis[]` (last value reused) and through multiple `SocketConnectHost`/`SocketConnectPort` endpoints on failure. `SocketConnectTimeout` (default 60s) for connect timeout.
- **Dynamic sessions (acceptor)**: `DynamicAcceptorSessionProvider` + `AcceptorSessionProvider` create `Session` objects on demand for incoming logons not pre-defined; gated by `DynamicSession` setting and `AcceptorTemplate`.
- **Proxy support (initiator)**: full HTTP and SOCKS proxy via Apache MINA `proxy` package (`ProtocolFactory.createIoProxyConnector`). Supports SOCKS 4 / 4a / 5 and HTTP 1.0/1.1 with optional Basic auth (`Proxy-Authorization` header), NTLM (`ProxyDomain`/`ProxyWorkstation`). Settings: `ProxyType`, `ProxyVersion`, `ProxyHost`, `ProxyPort`, `ProxyUser`, `ProxyPassword`, `ProxyDomain`, `ProxyWorkstation`. Wired through `InitiatorProxyIoHandler` → `IoSessionInitiator`.

### Minor gaps (non-blocking for parity comparison)
- Exact SSL keystore/truststore/cipher/protocol key strings inside `SSLSupport.java`/`SSLConfig.java` were not enumerated (only `SETTING_USE_SSL` confirmed); easy to read if needed.
- Detailed method-level behavior of `Session.next()`/resend state machine read only at signature level (lines 1277/1952/2125) rather than full bodies.
- `quickfixj-perf-test` / `quickfixj-stress-test` module internals not inspected (names imply JMH/perf + stress harnesses).

Evidence base: directory listings, `find` over core/base/mina/test dirs, `grep` of `SETTING_*` constants across Session.java/SessionFactory/Acceptor/Initiator/NetworkingOptions/JdbcSetting/FileStoreFactory/FileLogFactory/SLF4JLogFactory, `SessionSettings.java`/`SessionState.java`/`SessionSchedule.java`/`DefaultSessionSchedule.java` content, and `customising-quickfixj.md`.
  - `next_steps`: If deeper parity detail is needed, the highest-value follow-ups are: (1) read `SSLSupport.java` end-to-end to enumerate the exact SSL keystore/truststore/cipher/protocol key strings; (2) read `Session.java` `next(Message)`/`next()`/`disconnect()` method bodies (lines ~1277–2150, ~2775) to map the exact resend/gap-fill/test-request/sequence-reset state machine; (3) inspect `quickfixj-base/src/main/resources` dictionaries and a sample generated `quickfixj-messages-fix44` class to confirm codegen output shape; (4) skim `quickfixj-perf-test`/`quickfixj-stress-test` modules to characterize load-testing tooling. None of these block the current parity inventory.

  ### 4. Transport Layer (MINA-based)
  - Public connectors: `SocketAcceptor`, `SocketInitiator`, `ThreadedSocketAcceptor`, `ThreadedSocketInitiator`.
  - `quickfix/mina` package: `AbstractIoHandler`, `CompositeIoFilterChainBuilder`, `EventHandlingStrategy` (with `SingleThreadedEventHandlingStrategy` and `ThreadPerSessionEventHandlingStrategy` for single-vs-thread-per-session message dispatch), `IoSessionResponder`, `NetworkingOptions`, `ProtocolFactory`, `QueueTracker`/`QueueTrackers`/`WatermarkTracker` (backpressure/queue management), `SessionConnector`, `CriticalProtocolCodecException`.
  - `mina/acceptor`: `AbstractSocketAcceptor`, `AcceptorIoHandler`, `AcceptorSessionProvider`, **`DynamicAcceptorSessionProvider`** (dynamic session creation on incoming logon).
  - `mina/initiator`: `AbstractSocketInitiator`, `InitiatorIoHandler`, **`InitiatorProxyIoHandler`** (proxy support), `IoSessionInitiator` (manages reconnect interval array + multiple connect hosts/ports), `ConnectException`.
  - `mina/message`: `FIXMessageDecoder`, `FIXMessageEncoder`, `FIXProtocolCodecFactory`, `PatternMatcher`.
  - `mina/ssl`: `AcceptorSslFilter`, `InitiatorSslFilter`, `SSLConfig`, `SSLContextFactory`, `SSLSupport`, `X509TrustManagerWrapper`.
  
  ### 5. Message Store Layer
  `MessageStore` interface + factories: `MemoryStore`, `FileStore` (+ `FileStoreSync`, `FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs`), `CachedFileStore` (in-memory cache over file), `JdbcStore` (DB-backed, with `JdbcUtil`, `JdbcSetting`, configurable table names + connection pool), `SleepycatStore` (Berkeley DB / Oracle JE), `NoopStore` (stateless/no persistence). Plus `MessageQueue` abstraction with `InMemoryMessageQueue` and `BoundInMemoryMessageQueue` (bounded queue for backpressure) and their factories.
  
  ### 6. Log Layer
  `Log` interface + `AbstractLog` base. Implementations: `ScreenLog` (stdout/stderr), `FileLog` (separate event/incoming/outgoing files), `JdbcLog` (DB tables, configurable), `SLF4JLog` (delegates to SLF4J with per-category routing: event/error/incoming/outgoing categories), `CompositeLog` (multiplex to multiple logs). Plus `LocationAwareLogFactory`, `CompositeLogFactory`, `LogUtil`.
  
  ### 7. Dictionary / Message Model
  - `DataDictionary` (in base): parses FIX XML dictionaries, validates fields/groups/components, msg-type/tag lookups, field-type checking, required-field validation, custom/UDF support.
  - `Message` (base): FIX message with header/body/trailer `FieldMap`s, group iteration, parsing/serialization, header field handling, reverse routing.
  - `Field`/`FieldMap`/`Group`/`MessageComponent`: typed field hierarchy, ordered field storage, repeating groups, components.
  - `MessageCracker`: visitor base class auto-generating `onMessage(...)` overloads per typed message (used by Application callbacks).
  - `MessageFactory`/`DefaultMessageFactory`: creates typed messages by BeginString + MsgType, version-aware.
  - `DataDictionaryProvider`/`DefaultDataDictionaryProvider`: per-transport/per-app dictionary selection (needed for FIXT 1.1 session/app split).
  - `ValidationSettings`, `MessageUtils`, `MessageSessionUtils`.
  
  ### 8. Codegen / Typed Messages / Versions
  - `quickfixj-dictgenerator` module: `Generator`, `Message`, `Field`, `Component`, `ComponentComponent`, `ComponentField`, `Enum`, `MsgType`, `MsgTypeField`, `MsgTypeComponent`, `Repository` — generates typed Field/Component/Message Java classes from FIX XML dictionaries.
  - `quickfixj-codegenerator` Maven plugin (and Gradle usage documented in `customising-quickfixj.md`) — `MessageCodeGenerator` with `Task` (dictFile, messagePackage, fieldPackage, outputBaseDirectory).
  - Supported FIX versions (each a `quickfixj-messages-*` module): **FIX 4.0, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 5.0, 5.0sp1, 5.0sp2, FIX Latest, FIXT 1.1**, plus `quickfixj-messages-all` (aggregate).
  - FIX 4.0–5.0sp2 generated from QuickFIX XML dictionaries; **FIX Latest generated from a FIX Orchestra repository** via `quickfixj-orchestration` module. `generator.decimal` build property toggles `BigDecimal` vs `double` for Qty/decimal fields. Custom Rules-of-Engagement supported by editing dictionaries/orchestration.
  
  ### 9. Acceptance Test Definitions
  Located at `quickfixj-core/src/test/resources/quickfix/test/acceptance/definitions/` — **481 `.def` scenario files**, organized by version subdirs (`fix40`, `fix41`, `fix42`, `fix43`, `fix44`, `fix50`, `fixLatest`, plus `future/server/fix43`). Scenario categories: `server/` (core protocol: logon/logout, seqnum gaps, resend, reject, garbled msgs, comp-id checks, test requests, session reset — e.g. `1a_ValidLogon`, `2b_MsgSeqNumTooHigh`, `14a_BadField`, `20_SimultaneousResendRequest`, `ReverseRoute`, `SessionReset`), `lastMsgSeqNumProcessed/`, `nextExpectedMsgSeqNum/`, `rejectGarbledMessages/`, `resendRequestChunkSize/` (chunked resend, gap-fill vs resend), `timestamps/` (higher-resolution timestamps), `validateChecksum/`. Java harness under `src/test/java/quickfix/test/acceptance/` (+ `resynch/`).
  
  ### 10. Config Keys (SessionSettings)
  Config keys are NOT centralized — they live as `SETTING_*` string constants across classes. Comprehensive set found:
  - **Session (Session.java)**: `HeartBtInt`, `CheckLatency`, `CheckCompID`, `MaxLatency`, `TestRequestDelayMultiplier`, `HeartBeatTimeoutMultiplier`, `NonStopSession`, `StartDay`, `EndDay`, `TimeZone`, `StartTime`, `EndTime`, `Weekdays`, `UseDataDictionary`, `DataDictionary`, `TransportDataDictionary`, `AppDataDictionary`, `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder`, `ValidateUnorderedGroupFields`, `FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter`, `ValidateFieldsHaveValues`, `ValidateIncomingMessage`, `LogonTimeout`, `LogoutTimeout`, `LogonTag`, `ResetOnLogout`, `ValidateSequenceNumbers`, `ResetOnDisconnect`, `ResetOnError`, `DisconnectOnError`, `TimeStampPrecision`, `ValidateUserDefinedFields`, `ResetOnLogon`, `Description`, `RefreshOnLogon`, `SendRedundantResendRequests`, `PersistMessages`, `ClosedResendInterval`, `AllowUnknownMsgFields`, `DefaultApplVerID`, `DisableHeartBeatCheck`, `EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed`, `EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, `RejectGarbledMessage`, `RejectInvalidMessage`, `RejectMessageOnUnhandledException`, `RequiresOrigSendingTime`, `ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore`, `AllowedRemoteAddresses`, `LogMessageWhenSessionNotFound`, `ResendRequestChunkSize`, `MaxScheduledWriteRequests`, `ValidateChecksum`, `AllowPosDup`.
  - **SessionFactory**: `ConnectionType` (initiator/acceptor), `ContinueInitializationOnError`.
  - **Acceptor**: `SocketAcceptProtocol`, `SocketAcceptPort`, `SocketAcceptAddress`, `AcceptorTemplate`.
  - **Initiator**: `ReconnectInterval`, `SocketConnectProtocol`, `SocketConnectHost`, `SocketConnectPort`, `SocketConnectTimeout`, `SocketLocalHost`, `SocketLocalPort`, `ProxyType/ProxyVersion/ProxyHost/ProxyPort/ProxyUser/ProxyPassword/ProxyDomain/ProxyWorkstation`, `DynamicSession`.
  - **NetworkingOptions**: `SocketKeepAlive`, `SocketOobInline`, `SocketReceiveBufferSize`, `SocketReuseAddress`, `SocketSendBufferSize`, `SocketLinger`, `SocketTcpNoDelay`, `SocketTrafficClass`, `SocketSynchronousWrites`, `SocketSynchronousWriteTimeout`.
  - **FileStore**: `FileStorePath`, `FileStoreSync`, `FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs`.
  - **FileLog**: `FileLogPath`, `FileIncludeMilliseconds`, `FileIncludeTimeStampForMessages`, `FileLogHeartbeats`.
  - **SLF4JLog**: `SLF4JLogEventCategory`, `SLF4JLogErrorEventCategory`, `SLF4JLogIncomingMessageCategory`, `SLF4JLogOutgoingMessageCategory`, `SLF4JLogPrependSessionID`, `SLF4JLogHeartbeats`.
  - **JdbcSetting**: `JdbcDriver`, `JdbcURL`, `JdbcUser`, `JdbcPassword`, `JdbcStoreMessagesTableName`, `JdbcStoreSessionsTableName`, `JdbcDataSourceName`, `JndiContextFactory`, `JndiProviderURL`, `JdbcLogHeartBeats`, `JdbcLogOutgoingTable`, `JdbcLogIncomingTable`, `JdbcLogEventTable`, `JdbcSessionIdDefaultPropertyValue`, plus pool sizing (`JdbcMaxActiveConnection`, `JdbcMinIdleConnection`, `JdbcMaxConnectionLifeTime`, `JdbcConnectionTimeout`, `JdbcConnectionIdleTimeout`, `JdbcConnectionKeepaliveTime`, `JdbcConnectionTestQuery`).
  - **SSL**: `SSLSupport.SETTING_USE_SSL` (`UseSSL`) plus keystore/truststore/cipher/protocol settings in `SSLSupport`/`SSLConfig` (exact key strings not fully enumerated — see gap below).
  
  ### 11. Examples (quickfixj-examples)
  - **executor**: minimal acceptor — `Executor` + `Application` + `MarketDataProvider`; auto-fills any incoming NewOrderSingle with an ExecutionReport (sells to any buy).
  - **banzai**: Swing-GUI order-entry **initiator** client — `Banzai`/`BanzaiApplication` + UI (`BanzaiFrame`, `OrderEntryPanel`, `OrderTableModel`, `ExecutionTableModel`), supports New/Cancel/Replace, tracks orders/executions, `OrderSide`/`OrderType`/`OrderTIF` enums.
  - **ordermatch**: a simple in-memory **matching engine** — `Application`, `Main`, `Market`, `Order`, `OrderMatcher`, `IdGenerator`; matches orders between multiple sessions.
  
  ### 12. SSL/TLS Support
  Dedicated `mina/ssl` package: `SSLSupport` (config keys), `SSLConfig` (holds keystore/truststore/protocols/ciphers), `SSLContextFactory` (builds `SSLContext`), `InitiatorSslFilter` + `AcceptorSslFilter` (MINA SSL filters for each direction), `X509TrustManagerWrapper` (custom trust manager). Enabled per-session via `UseSSL=true`; acceptor groups sessions by SSL config + bind address in `AcceptorSocketDescriptor`.
  
  ### 13. Scheduling (Session Schedule)
  - `SessionSchedule` interface: `isSessionTime()`, `isSameSession(time1, time2)`, `isNonStopSession()`.
  - `DefaultSessionSchedule` supports **four modes**: non-stop (`NonStopSession=true`), daily (`StartTime`/`EndTime` only), weekly (`StartDay`/`EndDay` + times), and **multi-weekday** (`Weekdays=Sun,Mon,...` comma list parsed via `DayConverter`). Uses `TimeEndPoint`/`TimeInterval` inner classes with day-of-week + time + TimeZone. `DefaultSessionScheduleFactory` builds from settings. Settings: `StartTime`, `EndTime`, `StartDay`, `EndDay`, `Weekdays`, `TimeZone`, `NonStopSession`.
  
  ### 14. Reconnect, Dynamic Sessions, Proxy
  - **Reconnect**: `ReconnectInterval` accepts a comma-separated list → `int[]` of escalating intervals; `IoSessionInitiator` cycles through `reconnectIntervalInMillis[]` (last value reused) and through multiple `SocketConnectHost`/`SocketConnectPort` endpoints on failure. `SocketConnectTimeout` (default 60s) for connect timeout.
  - **Dynamic sessions (acceptor)**: `DynamicAcceptorSessionProvider` + `AcceptorSessionProvider` create `Session` objects on demand for incoming logons not pre-defined; gated by `DynamicSession` setting and `AcceptorTemplate`.
  - **Proxy support (initiator)**: full HTTP and SOCKS proxy via Apache MINA `proxy` package (`ProtocolFactory.createIoProxyConnector`). Supports SOCKS 4 / 4a / 5 and HTTP 1.0/1.1 with optional Basic auth (`Proxy-Authorization` header), NTLM (`ProxyDomain`/`ProxyWorkstation`). Settings: `ProxyType`, `ProxyVersion`, `ProxyHost`, `ProxyPort`, `ProxyUser`, `ProxyPassword`, `ProxyDomain`, `ProxyWorkstation`. Wired through `InitiatorProxyIoHandler` → `IoSessionInitiator`.
  
  ### Minor gaps (non-blocking for parity comparison)
  - Exact SSL keystore/truststore/cipher/protocol key strings inside `SSLSupport.java`/`SSLConfig.java` were not enumerated (only `SETTING_USE_SSL` confirmed); easy to read if needed.
  - Detailed method-level behavior of `Session.next()`/resend state machine read only at signature level (lines 1277/1952/2125) rather than full bodies.
  - `quickfixj-perf-test` / `quickfixj-stress-test` module internals not inspected (names imply JMH/perf + stress harnesses).
  
  Evidence base: directory listings, `find` over core/base/mina/test dirs, `grep` of `SETTING_*` constants across Session.java/SessionFactory/Acceptor/Initiator/NetworkingOptions/JdbcSetting/FileStoreFactory/FileLogFactory/SLF4JLogFactory, `SessionSettings.java`/`SessionState.java`/`SessionSchedule.java`/`DefaultSessionSchedule.java` content, and `customising-quickfixj.md`.
  
  Next steps:
  If deeper parity detail is needed, the highest-value follow-ups are: (1) read `SSLSupport.java` end-to-end to enumerate the exact SSL keystore/truststore/cipher/protocol key strings; (2) read `Session.java` `next(Message)`/`next()`/`disconnect()` method bodies (lines ~1277–2150, ~2775) to map the exact resend/gap-fill/test-request/sequence-reset state machine; (3) inspect `quickfixj-base/src/main/resources` dictionaries and a sample generated `quickfixj-messages-fix44` class to confirm codegen output shape; (4) skim `quickfixj-perf-test`/`quickfixj-stress-test` modules to characterize load-testing tooling. # 1. truefix-core — FULLY IMPLEMENTED
Files: `src/{lib,error,field,tags,group,message,field_map,cracker,factory}.rs`, `src/codec/{mod,encode,decode}.rs`, `tests/{reference_vectors,roundtrip,field_types,groups,garbled}.rs`.

Public types:
- `Field` { tag: u32, value: Vec<u8> } — `new`, `string`, `int`, `tag`, `value_bytes`, `as_str`, `as_int`, `as_decimal`, `as_char`, `as_bool`, `as_utc_timestamp`. Timestamp parser accepts ms→picosecond, truncates to ns.
- `FieldError` enum: NotUtf8/NotInt/NotDecimal/NotChar/NotBool/NotTimestamp (all carry `tag`).
- `DecodeError` enum: Empty, MissingBeginString, InvalidBodyLength, BodyLengthMismatch{declared,actual}, MissingChecksum, ChecksumMismatch{declared,computed}, GarbledField{offset,reason}, Truncated{offset}, InvalidTag{offset}.
- `FieldMap` (ordered; `Member` enum = Field | Group{count_tag,entries}): `new`, `add_field`, `set`, `get`, `contains`, `add_group`, `group`, `fields`, (`members()` pub(crate)).
- `Group` {count_tag, entries}: `new`, `add_entry`, `count_tag`, `len`, `is_empty`, (`into_parts` pub(crate)).
- `Message` { pub header/body/trailer: FieldMap }: `new`, `begin_string`, `msg_type`, `encode`, `decode`.
- `tags`: const `SOH=0x01`, `BEGIN_STRING=8`, `BODY_LENGTH=9`, `MSG_TYPE=35`, `CHECK_SUM=10`, `MSG_SEQ_NUM=34`, `SENDER_COMP_ID=49`, `TARGET_COMP_ID=56`, `SENDING_TIME=52`; fns `is_header`, `is_trailer`, `data_field_for_length` (13 length→data tag mappings, e.g. 95→96).
- `codec::encode(msg)->Vec<u8>`: emits 8,9,35,header,body,trailer,10; computes BodyLength + CheckSum mod 256.
- `codec::decode(input)->Result<Message,DecodeError>`: tokenizes honoring length-prefixed data fields (embedded SOH safe), verifies BodyLength + CheckSum, routes fields to header/body/trailer. **Decoding yields flat fields (no dict-driven group parsing).**
- Traits `MessageCracker` { type Error; crack(&mut self,&Message)->Result<(),Error> } and `MessageFactory` { create(&self,&str,&str)->Message } — **skeleton contracts only**; docs say typed dispatch/codegen arrives in Stage S4.

Config keys: none.
Tests: T099 (reference QF/J wire vectors decode+roundtrip byte-identical, rawdata preserved); T010 (known vector roundtrip, BodyLength=21/CheckSum=086, programmatic NewOrderSingle roundtrip); T009 (field-type conversions + bad-conversion errors + ns/ps timestamp truncation + invalid timestamps); T012 (group build→known vector, empty group→count 0, nested group byte-level roundtrip); T011 (negative codec: empty, missing begin string, invalid tag, checksum/body-length mismatch, truncation, missing '=', never-panics on all 256 byte values).
Deferred: dict-driven group *parsing* from flat wire (group *building/encoding* works today).

Methods: `new`, `set_dictionary`, `id`, `state`, `is_logged_on`, `next_out_seq`, `next_in_seq`, `seed_sequences`, `handle(Event)->Vec<Action>`, `send_app(Message)`.

- `SessionConfig` struct (pub fields): begin_string, sender_comp_id, target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval(30), role, reset_on_logon(true), reset_on_logout(false), reset_on_disconnect(false), refresh_on_logon(false), resend_request_chunk_size(0), enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num(false), check_latency(true), max_latency(120). `new(...)`, `session_id()`.

- `Application` async trait (`async_trait`): on_create, on_logon, on_logout, to_admin, from_admin (->Result<(),String>), to_app, from_app (->Result<(),String>); all default no-ops.
- `admin` (pub(crate)) builders: logon, logout, heartbeat, test_request, resend_request, sequence_reset, reject, reject_with_reason, business_message_reject, prepare_resend (stamps PossDupFlag=Y + OrigSendingTime + refreshes SendingTime).
- `tags` (pub(crate) consts): 34,43,52,122,36,123,7,16,141,789,112,58,45,372,373,380,98,108.
- `time_util::now_utc_timestamp()` -> `YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.mmm`.

Behavior implemented: initiator sends logon on connect; acceptor waits + responds; logon ResetSeqNumFlag handling; heartbeat after idle tick; test-request answered with heartbeat; logout bilateral + unsolicited; silent-peer disconnect threshold (2*hb+2); **sequence gap recovery** (high seq → queue + ResendRequest, chunked via resend_request_chunk_size); SequenceReset gap-fill & reset modes; low-seq handling (PossDup ignored, else logout+disconnect); **ResendRequest fulfillment** (resent app msgs get PossDup+OrigSendingTime; admin msgs gap-filled); NextExpectedMsgSeqNum(789) on logon; **dictionary validation hook** (`validate_app`) → emits Reject (session-level, 35=3) or BusinessMessageReject (business-level, 35=j).

Config keys recognized: the `SessionConfig` fields above (programmatic only).
Tests: T023 (state transitions: initiator/acceptor logon, test-request→heartbeat, idle heartbeat, logout, unsolicited logout, silent-peer disconnect); T032–T035 (high-seq resend+queue, gap-fill then queued processed, low-seq with/without PossDup, resend app+gapfill admin with PossDup/OrigSendingTime, chunked resend caps EndSeqNo, SequenceReset reset mode, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum on logon); T052 (dict validation wired in: valid app accepted, invalid Side→Reject(373=5), unknown MsgType→BusinessMessageReject(35=j), admin not dict-rejected).
Gaps/deferred: persistence beyond in-memory store (state.rs doc); scheduling; **`check_latency`/`max_latency` config fields exist but are NOT enforced** (no SendingTime-vs-clock check in `on_received`). `refresh_on_logon` field exists but not acted on in session.

# 3. truefix-transport — FULLY IMPLEMENTED (S2–S5)
Files: `src/{lib,framing}.rs`, `tests/integration_logon.rs`.

Public types:
- `Services` { store: Option<Arc<dyn MessageStore>>, log: Option<Arc<dyn Log>> } (Clone, Default).
- `SessionHandle` { control, task }: `logout()`, `join()`.
- `Acceptor<A: Application>`: `bind`, `bind_with`, `local_addr`, `serve()->JoinHandle`.
- Free fns `connect_initiator(addr,config,app)`, `connect_initiator_with(addr,config,app,services)`.
- `framing::frame_length(buf)->Result<Option<usize>,DecodeError>` (pub(crate)): locates message via BodyLength + fixed 7-byte `10=XXX<SOH>` trailer.

Behavior: tokio TCP initiator/acceptor; reads into buffer, `frame_length` splits messages, `decode`s, dispatches to sans-IO `Session`; runs 1s ticker for `Event::Tick`; control channel for graceful logout; restores sequence numbers from store on connect (`seed_sequences`) and persists them after each dispatch; routes inbound through `from_admin`/`from_app` and outbound through `to_admin`/`to_app`; logs incoming/outgoing/events. `recv_control` uses `pending()` once channel closes to avoid busy-loop. Garbled frames are **dropped** (comment notes RejectGarbledMessage "arrives in S3").
Tests: T022 — two-task loopback logon→heartbeat→graceful-logout on FIX.4.2 and FIX.4.4 (multi_thread tokio). framing has inline tests (complete frame, needs-more-bytes, two messages in buffer).
Deferred: multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, TLS; RejectGarbledMessage for garbled frames.

Files: `build.rs`, `src/{lib,fixt,model,parser,validate,hash}.rs`, `tests/{parse,fixt,dual_track,toggles,rejection_layers}.rs`. Bundled sources: `dict-src/normalized/{FIX44,FIXT11,FIX50}.fixdict`.

- `FieldDef` {tag, name, field_type, values:Vec<String>}: `allows(&str)`.
- `MessageDef` {msg_type, name, required:Vec<u32>, optional:Vec<u32>}: `allows_tag(u32)`.
- `DataDictionary` {version, fields, field_by_name, messages, header, trailer, hash}: `version`, `hash`, `field(tag)`, `field_by_name(name)`, `message(msg_type)`, `is_header`, `is_trailer`, `field_count`, `message_count`.
- `RejectReason` enum (InvalidTagNumber=0, RequiredTagMissing=1, TagNotDefinedForMessageType=2, UndefinedTag=3, TagSpecifiedWithoutValue=4, ValueIsIncorrect=5, IncorrectDataFormat=6, InvalidMsgType=11): `code()->u32`.

- `ParseError` enum: MissingVersion, Malformed{line,reason:&'static str}, UnknownType{line,token}.
- `parse(&str)->Result<DataDictionary,ParseError>`: parses directives `version`, `field <tag> <name> <TYPE> [enum vals...]`, `header <tags>`, `trailer <tags>`, `message <msgtype> <name> req:.. opt:..`; `#` comments; computes FNV-1a hash of source.
- `FixtDictionaries` {transport, applications:BTreeMap, default_appl_ver_id?}: `new`, `with_application`, `with_default_appl_ver_id`, `transport()`, `application_for(Option<&str>)`.
- `DataDictionary::validate(&Message,&ValidationOptions)->Result<(),ValidationError>` (validate.rs): checks empty values, unknown tags (UDF ≥5000 skipped unless toggle), field-type format, enum membership, field belongs to header/trailer/message, required fields present. Unknown-but-structural MsgType → business reject; field/validation failures → session reject.
- `load_fix44/load_fixt11/load_fix50()`; const sources `FIX44_DICT/FIXT11_DICT/FIX50_DICT`.
- `build.rs`: generates `FIX44_DICT_HASH/FIXT11_DICT_HASH/FIX50_DICT_HASH` (FNV-1a) + `fix44_msgs/fixt11_msgs/fix50_msgs` modules of MsgType constants (e.g. `LOGON="A"`, `NEWORDERSINGLE="D"`); included via `include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"),"/generated.rs"))`.
- `hash::fnv1a` (duplicated in build.rs by design).

Config keys: `ValidationOptions` toggles above.
Tests: T043 (parse FIX44: version, Symbol=55, Side=Char, Price, NewOrderSingle/Logon defs, header/trailer membership, counts; enum values; required/optional fields; missing-version & unknown-type errors); T046 (FIXT split: transport≠application, DefaultApplVerID fallback, explicit vs unknown ApplVerID, none→None); T047 (dual-track: codegen hash == runtime hash for all 3 dicts; generated MsgType constants present); T044 (one test per toggle: valid passes, required missing, unknown tag rejected unless allowed, UDF skipped unless validated, bad enum→ValueIsIncorrect, bad format→IncorrectDataFormat, tag-not-for-msgtype, empty value); T045 (two rejection layers: dict failure=session-level, unknown MsgType=business-level, missing MsgType=session-level).

# 7. truefix-config — PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED (parsing only)
File: `src/lib.rs` (inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`).

Public types:
- `ConfigError` enum: Malformed{line,text}, UnresolvedVariable{line,name}.
- `SessionSettings` {default: BTreeMap<String,String>, sessions: Vec<BTreeMap<String,String>>}: `parse(&str)`, `default_section()`, `sessions()`.
- Parses QuickFIX-style `[DEFAULT]`/`[SESSION]` dialect: defaults inherited by every session, per-session overrides, `#` comments (full-line + trailing), `${name}` interpolation (resolved against default then merged session), blank lines ignored.

**Recognized config keys: none validated** — any `key=value` string is accepted into a `BTreeMap<String,String>`. Examples in tests: `ConnectionType`, `HeartBtInt`, `BeginString`, `SenderCompID`, `Host`, `Port`, `Endpoint`.
**Key gap/stub:** the doc explicitly states *"The full Appendix A key surface and the mapping to `SessionConfig` are filled in across later stages."* So **`SessionSettings` is NOT mapped to `SessionConfig`** — it only produces string maps. This is the one genuine incomplete piece across the surveyed crates.
Tests (inline): default-inherited + session override; `${Host}:${Port}` interpolation; comments/blank lines ignored; unresolved variable errors; malformed line errors.

  # 1. truefix-core — FULLY IMPLEMENTED
  Files: `src/{lib,error,field,tags,group,message,field_map,cracker,factory}.rs`, `src/codec/{mod,encode,decode}.rs`, `tests/{reference_vectors,roundtrip,field_types,groups,garbled}.rs`.
  
  Public types:
  - `Field` { tag: u32, value: Vec<u8> } — `new`, `string`, `int`, `tag`, `value_bytes`, `as_str`, `as_int`, `as_decimal`, `as_char`, `as_bool`, `as_utc_timestamp`. Timestamp parser accepts ms→picosecond, truncates to ns.
  - `FieldError` enum: NotUtf8/NotInt/NotDecimal/NotChar/NotBool/NotTimestamp (all carry `tag`).
  - `DecodeError` enum: Empty, MissingBeginString, InvalidBodyLength, BodyLengthMismatch{declared,actual}, MissingChecksum, ChecksumMismatch{declared,computed}, GarbledField{offset,reason}, Truncated{offset}, InvalidTag{offset}.
  - `FieldMap` (ordered; `Member` enum = Field | Group{count_tag,entries}): `new`, `add_field`, `set`, `get`, `contains`, `add_group`, `group`, `fields`, (`members()` pub(crate)).
  - `Group` {count_tag, entries}: `new`, `add_entry`, `count_tag`, `len`, `is_empty`, (`into_parts` pub(crate)).
  - `Message` { pub header/body/trailer: FieldMap }: `new`, `begin_string`, `msg_type`, `encode`, `decode`.
  - `tags`: const `SOH=0x01`, `BEGIN_STRING=8`, `BODY_LENGTH=9`, `MSG_TYPE=35`, `CHECK_SUM=10`, `MSG_SEQ_NUM=34`, `SENDER_COMP_ID=49`, `TARGET_COMP_ID=56`, `SENDING_TIME=52`; fns `is_header`, `is_trailer`, `data_field_for_length` (13 length→data tag mappings, e.g. 95→96).
  - `codec::encode(msg)->Vec<u8>`: emits 8,9,35,header,body,trailer,10; computes BodyLength + CheckSum mod 256.
  - `codec::decode(input)->Result<Message,DecodeError>`: tokenizes honoring length-prefixed data fields (embedded SOH safe), verifies BodyLength + CheckSum, routes fields to header/body/trailer. **Decoding yields flat fields (no dict-driven group parsing).**
  - Traits `MessageCracker` { type Error; crack(&mut self,&Message)->Result<(),Error> } and `MessageFactory` { create(&self,&str,&str)->Message } — **skeleton contracts only**; docs say typed dispatch/codegen arrives in Stage S4.
  
  Config keys: none.
  Tests: T099 (reference QF/J wire vectors decode+roundtrip byte-identical, rawdata preserved); T010 (known vector roundtrip, BodyLength=21/CheckSum=086, programmatic NewOrderSingle roundtrip); T009 (field-type conversions + bad-conversion errors + ns/ps timestamp truncation + invalid timestamps); T012 (group build→known vector, empty group→count 0, nested group byte-level roundtrip); T011 (negative codec: empty, missing begin string, invalid tag, checksum/body-length mismatch, truncation, missing '=', never-panics on all 256 byte values).
  Deferred: dict-driven group *parsing* from flat wire (group *building/encoding* works today).

Methods: `new`, `set_dictionary`, `id`, `state`, `is_logged_on`, `next_out_seq`, `next_in_seq`, `seed_sequences`, `handle(Event)->Vec<Action>`, `send_app(Message)`.

  - `SessionId` {begin_string, sender_comp_id, sender_sub_id?, sender_location_id?, target_comp_id, target_sub_id?, target_location_id?, session_qualifier?}: `new`, `Display` (`Begin:Sender->Target[:qual]`).
  - `Application` async trait (`async_trait`): on_create, on_logon, on_logout, to_admin, from_admin (->Result<(),String>), to_app, from_app (->Result<(),String>); all default no-ops.
  - `admin` (pub(crate)) builders: logon, logout, heartbeat, test_request, resend_request, sequence_reset, reject, reject_with_reason, business_message_reject, prepare_resend (stamps PossDupFlag=Y + OrigSendingTime + refreshes SendingTime).
  - `tags` (pub(crate) consts): 34,43,52,122,36,123,7,16,141,789,112,58,45,372,373,380,98,108.
  - `time_util::now_utc_timestamp()` -> `YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.mmm`.
  
  Behavior implemented: initiator sends logon on connect; acceptor waits + responds; logon ResetSeqNumFlag handling; heartbeat after idle tick; test-request answered with heartbeat; logout bilateral + unsolicited; silent-peer disconnect threshold (2*hb+2); **sequence gap recovery** (high seq → queue + ResendRequest, chunked via resend_request_chunk_size); SequenceReset gap-fill & reset modes; low-seq handling (PossDup ignored, else logout+disconnect); **ResendRequest fulfillment** (resent app msgs get PossDup+OrigSendingTime; admin msgs gap-filled); NextExpectedMsgSeqNum(789) on logon; **dictionary validation hook** (`validate_app`) → emits Reject (session-level, 35=3) or BusinessMessageReject (business-level, 35=j).
  
  Config keys recognized: the `SessionConfig` fields above (programmatic only).
  Tests: T023 (state transitions: initiator/acceptor logon, test-request→heartbeat, idle heartbeat, logout, unsolicited logout, silent-peer disconnect); T032–T035 (high-seq resend+queue, gap-fill then queued processed, low-seq with/without PossDup, resend app+gapfill admin with PossDup/OrigSendingTime, chunked resend caps EndSeqNo, SequenceReset reset mode, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum on logon); T052 (dict validation wired in: valid app accepted, invalid Side→Reject(373=5), unknown MsgType→BusinessMessageReject(35=j), admin not dict-rejected).
  Gaps/deferred: persistence beyond in-memory store (state.rs doc); scheduling; **`check_latency`/`max_latency` config fields exist but are NOT enforced** (no SendingTime-vs-clock check in `on_received`). `refresh_on_logon` field exists but not acted on in session.

  # 3. truefix-transport — FULLY IMPLEMENTED (S2–S5)
  Files: `src/{lib,framing}.rs`, `tests/integration_logon.rs`.
  
  Public types:
  - `Services` { store: Option<Arc<dyn MessageStore>>, log: Option<Arc<dyn Log>> } (Clone, Default).
  - `SessionHandle` { control, task }: `logout()`, `join()`.
  - `Acceptor<A: Application>`: `bind`, `bind_with`, `local_addr`, `serve()->JoinHandle`.
  - Free fns `connect_initiator(addr,config,app)`, `connect_initiator_with(addr,config,app,services)`.
  - `framing::frame_length(buf)->Result<Option<usize>,DecodeError>` (pub(crate)): locates message via BodyLength + fixed 7-byte `10=XXX<SOH>` trailer.
  
  Behavior: tokio TCP initiator/acceptor; reads into buffer, `frame_length` splits messages, `decode`s, dispatches to sans-IO `Session`; runs 1s ticker for `Event::Tick`; control channel for graceful logout; restores sequence numbers from store on connect (`seed_sequences`) and persists them after each dispatch; routes inbound through `from_admin`/`from_app` and outbound through `to_admin`/`to_app`; logs incoming/outgoing/events. `recv_control` uses `pending()` once channel closes to avoid busy-loop. Garbled frames are **dropped** (comment notes RejectGarbledMessage "arrives in S3").
  Tests: T022 — two-task loopback logon→heartbeat→graceful-logout on FIX.4.2 and FIX.4.4 (multi_thread tokio). framing has inline tests (complete frame, needs-more-bytes, two messages in buffer).
  Deferred: multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, TLS; RejectGarbledMessage for garbled frames.

  - `CachedFileStore(FileStore)`: shares FileStore impl (write-batching optimizations deferred to S5).
  - `NoopStore`: seqs always 1, saves discarded, gets empty.
  - `SqlStore` (cfg `sql`, SQLite via sqlx): `connect(url)`; tables `seqnums(id=0 PK, sender, target)` + `messages(seq PK, data BLOB)`.
  - `StoreConfig` enum: Memory, File{dir}, CachedFile{dir}, Noop, `#[cfg(sql)]` Sql{url}.
  - `build_store(&StoreConfig)->Box<dyn MessageStore>`.
  
  Config keys: `StoreConfig` variants above.
  Tests: T053/T060 — memory round-trip; file survives restart (seqs + messages); cached file survives restart; noop inert; **corrupted trailing record recovered** (was_corrupted=true, good prefix intact); reset clears; factory builds each backend; (cfg sql) sql survives restart.

  Files: `src/{lib,file,composite,screen,tracing_log}.rs`, `tests/composite.rs`.
  
  Public types:
  - `LogError` enum: Io(String).
  - `Log` trait (Send+Sync, infallible/best-effort): `on_incoming(&str)`, `on_outgoing(&str)`, `on_event(&str)`.
  - `ScreenLog`: messages→stdout, events→stderr with `<incoming>/<outgoing>/<event>` prefixes.
  - `FileLog` {messages:Mutex<File>, events:Mutex<File>}: `open(dir)` → `messages.log` + `event.log` (append). Writes `I `/`O ` prefixed message lines, bare event lines.
  - `TracingLog`: emits via `tracing::info!` with targets `truefix::message`/`truefix::event`.
  - `CompositeLog` {logs:Vec<Box<dyn Log>>}: fan-out.
  - `LogConfig` enum: Screen, File{dir}, Tracing, Composite(Vec<LogConfig>).
  - `build_log(&LogConfig)->Box<dyn Log>`.
  
  Config keys: `LogConfig` variants above.
